{{Quote|Do you think bloodshed could have been avoided in the Gotal hostage standoff?|A [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] "Quick Poll"|<ref name="Quick Poll">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|53||Quick Poll}}</ref>}}
The '''Gotal hostage standoff''', one of the conflicts in the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], took place on [[Atzerri/Legends|Atzerri]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].

==Background==
After the [[Battle of Antar 4 (Separatist Crisis)|Battle of Antar 4]], hundreds of [[Gotal/Legends|Gotals]] fled their homeworld of [[Antar 4/Legends|Antar 4]] for the nearby planet Atzerri. Among them were fugitive members of the [[Roshu Sune/Legends|Roshu Sune]], a radical splinter group of the [[Gotal Assembly for Separation/Legends|Gotal Assembly for Separation]]. Due to the lax security at Atzerri's spaceports, these wanted criminals had little trouble hiding on the busy trading planet.<ref name="Guerillas" />

When [[Emissary/Legends|Emissary]] [[Nathanjo Nirrelz]], a Gotal [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] from Antar 4, visited Atzerri on a mission to secure [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] supplies, he was followed by Roshu Sune agents after his arrival at the [[Skreeka]] spaceport. After he boarded a [[Passenger liner/Legends|commuter hopper]] to the Atzerri city of [[Talos]], five Gotals hijacked the vessel and forced it to land in a nearby swamp. The hostage takers demanded that the Gotal government sign the Roshu Sune's articles of secession in exchange for Nirrelz and the other hostages.<ref name="Guerillas" />

The Gotal government tried to stop outsiders from interfering with what they saw as an internal affair.<ref name="Guerillas" /> [[Gotal Foreign Affairs Commune]] leader [[Shagrad Loset]] later described the standoff as "a Gotal issue, best handled by Gotal." Despite this, the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] sent a six-[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] team, led by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Sarrissa Jeng/Legends|Sarrissa Jeng]], to resolve the situation. According to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]], the Jedi were there to negotiate a peaceful solution.<ref name="Standoff" />

==The confrontation==
The Jedi team arrived in the Atzerri swamps, and began to negotiate with the Roshu Sune. The negotiations were cut short when unknown parties in the nearby swamps began to fire their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. Meanwhile, the Roshu Sune agents inside the hopper, apparently in a state of panic, started to fire on their hostages. Two Jedi, [[Antyard-Wo Shissan]] and [[Kaloor Cofi]], were killed in the crossfire.<ref name="Standoff" />

The remaining Jedi stormed the hopper, in an attempt to save the surviving hostages. Although one of the guerrillas set off a [[thermite gel bomb]] which set the hopper aflame, the Jedi managed to rescue thirteen hostages. They were, however, forced to kill the hostage takers.<ref name="Standoff" />

==Aftermath==
The wounded hostages, most of whom suffered from burns and smoke inhalation, were rushed to hospitals in Skreeka and Talos. The bodies of the slain Jedi were taken to the Jedi chapter house on [[Aleen/Legends|Aleen]] for their funerals.<ref name="Standoff" />

Sarrissa Jeng, a [[Unidentified Mon Calamari Jedi Knight|Mon Calamari Jedi Knight]], and two other Jedi survived the standoff. Nathanjo Nirrelz and twelve other hostages (including [[Palhra Wavren]], a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]], and a [[Bith/Legends|Bith]]) also survived. While a grateful Wavren said it was a "miracle" that half of the hostages survived, Shagrad Loset criticized the Jedi's handling of the crisis, describing it as "[[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] diplomacy."<ref name="Standoff" /> The incident was controversial with the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], with 36% of those responding to a [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] poll expressing the opinion that bloodshed could have been avoided.<ref name="Quick Poll" />
